originalTitle: Over 1,800 killed since junta seized power in Burkina Faso, rights group says
title: Over 1,800 killed since junta seized power in Burkina Faso, rights group says
content: Human Rights Watch says Capt Traoré, other military leaders and jihadists "may be liable" for the killings.

originalTitle: Explosions at Burundi ammunition depot kill 13 civilians - army
title: Explosions at Burundi ammunition depot kill 13 civilians - army
content: Powerful blasts destroyed homes and sparked panic in the city of Bujumbura on Tuesday night.

originalTitle: South African army arrive in crime hotspots to help tackle gangs
title: South African army arrive in crime hotspots to help tackle gangs
content: The army has been deployed to five of South Africa's nine provinces for a year-long operation.

originalTitle: Israeli strikes kill 7 in southern Lebanon, Hezbollah targets ground troops
title: Israeli strikes kill 7 in southern Lebanon, Hezbollah targets ground troops
content: Fighting in southern Lebanon continues as Israel seeks to establish a 'security zone' and dismantle Hezbollah.

originalTitle: How Israel’s record budget will finance expanding illegal settlements
title: How Israel’s record budget will finance expanding illegal settlements
content: Budget of $271bn comes against a backdrop of surging violence by Israeli settlers towards Palestinians in the West Bank.

originalTitle: Libyan military leader may have combat drones despite UN embargo
title: Libyan military leader may have combat drones despite UN embargo
content: Reuters news agency says satellite images show ⁠at least three drones at Al Khadim airbase between April and December.

originalTitle: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
content: There were some glaring omissions in the president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

originalTitle: 'Oh my goodness, that is spectacular!': See BBC science editor react to launch
title: 'Oh my goodness, that is spectacular!': See BBC science editor react to launch
content: Nasa's Artemis II has blasted off and is now orbiting Earth, before continuing on to circle the Moon.

originalTitle: Man dies in storm as Saharan dust shrouds Crete
title: Man dies in storm as Saharan dust shrouds Crete
content: A man dies in flooding near Athens as rain lashes several regions in Greece, while a Saharan dust storm enveloped Crete.

originalTitle: Isak to return to training this week - Slot
title: Isak to return to training this week - Slot
content: British record signing Alexander Isak will return to Liverpool training on Thursday, head coach Arne Slot confirms.

originalTitle: Officials correct not to give Amad penalty - panel
title: Officials correct not to give Amad penalty - panel
content: The Premier League's KMI Panel says both referee and VAR were right not to give a penalty to Manchester United's Amad Diallo at Bournemouth.

originalTitle: Who uses academy players most in Premier League - and does it matter?
title: Who uses academy players most in Premier League - and does it matter?
content: As Max Dowman becomes the latest academy product to make an impact in the Premier League, BBC Sport looks at who uses their youngsters most.
